112483.explanation: The focus is on the quality of the members of the executive board, not their gender.

112484.explanation: An important topic such as: independent governance or merger with another municipality would be well-suited for a referendum.

112487.explanation: This possibility is always open for discussion.

112489.explanation: There is no space for that in Doesburg.

112493.explanation: Rules like these encourage government paternalism and do not belong at the municipal level.

112498.explanation: This is a responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves.

112500.explanation: The foundation Leergeld (now SUN Youth) was established for this purpose.

112503.explanation: Music lessons are part of cultural development. Culture in a general sense is important and the municipality already supports it.

112509.explanation: This need should first be made clear.

112511.explanation: This desire definitely exists and we want to bring this proposal back to the council in the coming period.

112515.explanation: But people who are able to do so could contribute to our beautiful city in this way.

112516.explanation: However, abuse must not be allowed. Enforcement is important here.

112519.explanation: This is not standard procedure, but can be done if there is a need for it.

112521.explanation: This must be national policy. Care always comes first, regardless of culture, beliefs, or religion.

112523.explanation: Property tax should not be a leftover item in a budget.

112526.explanation: Employment is important for Doesburg. If the opportunities exist, then that is possible.

112527.explanation: If there is a need for it, then that is fine.

112530.explanation: Let's first cover the roofs (for example of barns) with solar panels instead of using valuable agricultural land.

112531.explanation: Preserving the swimming pool is important. Providing more funding can also be done, for example, in the form of a subsidized loan. Doesburg must cherish its facilities.

112533.explanation: Doesburg is too small a municipality for that, and experience from the past showed that there was no support for it.

112540.explanation: This might be possible in the shopping streets; however, the goods need to be delivered.

112541.explanation: Doesburg can remain an independent municipality by cooperating well (joint arrangements) with other municipalities on general policy areas. Financially, Doesburg is a healthy municipality with a structurally balanced budget.

112542.explanation: Doesburg needs young people and young families.

112543.explanation: Traffic flow is more important than long-term parking. Free parking is still possible before 10:00 and after 18:00. At the Turfhaven, the Bleek, and the Maurits site, parking is free.

112483.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

112484.explanation: Local democracy is important and everyone should be able to make their voice heard. Referendums can be a good way, as well as citizens' assemblies or other forms.

112487.explanation: In Doesburg, it is almost always allowed in the rural area to have, for example, a farm shop, a small campsite, or caravan storage.

112489.explanation: Doesburg has a small own territory which we must manage wisely. A distribution center creates little-poorly paid-employment and is unsightly in the landscape.

112491.explanation: The starting point is that these employers themselves are and remain responsible for this.

112493.explanation: Most people enjoy lighting fires, but many people suffer from it as well. We must take each other into account. Therefore, the burning guide must be followed so people do not suffer from smoke and fine particles. We also believe enforcement belongs to this.

112498.explanation: Accessibility is very important for people with disabilities or who are simply not very mobile. We have worked on this in recent years. Entrepreneurs often cannot simply afford such adjustments. Therefore, we believe the municipality should assist.

112499.explanation: In principle, this must be possible but under conditions, for example, preserving the small-scale agricultural landscape and no larger footprint than the existing structures/buildings.

112500.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

112503.explanation: Music and other creative subjects are good for children's development and also serve as a good outlet, since children are under pressure from, among other things, social media. We want music lessons to be accessible not only to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

112509.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A women's shelter is indispensable, also for our municipality. But Doesburg is too small to organize this by itself and must arrange it in consultation with neighboring municipalities.

112511.explanation: People often want to stay living in their own environment. Within current regulations, that is also possible. But sometimes people must move to provide informal care or for work, and they too need a place to live. We are satisfied with the current regional cooperation via 'entree.nu'.

112513.explanation: In Doesburg, apart from CapFun, we have few vacation homes. We believe these homes should not be converted into permanent residences. Doesburg and the surrounding Veluwe area are important vacation areas. Removing vacation homes causes a different problem.

112515.explanation: If people with welfare benefits want and can do meaningful (volunteer) work, the municipality should encourage and facilitate that. We do not believe it should be mandatory. A stable base, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality help to participate in society again.

112516.explanation: This causes people with the lowest incomes in our municipality to face great problems. They cannot spare this money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water. Moreover, abolishing it will cause more families to fall into debt and create another problem for the municipality.

112517.explanation: People and families with an income below 120% of the welfare level should qualify for help and support.

112519.explanation: The future is that there will be more and more electric cars. That is also necessary because of the climate. The municipality must prepare for this.

112521.explanation: In principle, it is best when a caregiver understands and takes into account what is important to you as a client regarding faith, culture, and beliefs. This should also be a fixed part of the training.

112523.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is the municipality’s own fund with which it can do things for residents, for example supporting the swimming pool. Raise it if necessary to maintain facilities, lower it if possible.

112526.explanation: The existing industrial areas must be modernized and made more sustainable. Especially companies that provide services or sell products to residents should be able to find a place on the industrial sites, such as car mechanics, contractors, or business services.

112528.explanation: Just like other vulnerable groups such as informal caregivers, we believe status holders should also have priority when allocating housing. The solution is to build more houses in the Netherlands so everyone can get a suitable home.

112530.explanation: Under conditions, solar panels can be placed on agricultural land. The framework adopted by the municipal council in 2025 contains the conditions with which we agree. Generating renewable energy is necessary to achieve our climate goals.

112531.explanation: Swimming pool Den Helder receives financial support from the municipality and that must continue. We find it important that Doesburg retains its swimming pool, especially for the growing group of elderly.

112540.explanation: The center should also be car-free in winter just like in summer.

112541.explanation: It is already not feasible and affordable to be an independent municipality. We believe the people of Doesburg must be able to participate in discussions and decisions about matters important to Doesburg after the merger. This can be done by establishing a City Council.

112542.explanation: For starters, seniors who want to downsize, and one- and two-person households, appropriate smaller and affordable homes must be built. Homes costing 200,000 to 300,000 euros for people with low to moderate incomes. The municipality should take a much more leading role in this.

112543.explanation: The rates for resident parking permits must be affordable and balanced. However, visitor parking rates are not very high. There is also plenty of free parking outside those areas. Construction and maintenance cost the community money, and in our opinion, the user should pay.

112483.explanation: Nice if this is possible; otherwise, the preference goes to the best candidate.

112484.explanation: Citizen participation is very important, provided they are well informed by the government.

112487.explanation: Farmers should have the freedom to operate enterprises in the rural area.

112489.explanation: We want to prevent overbuilding because Doesburg is a city with historic character.

112491.explanation: We do not think living where you work is a good idea.

112493.explanation: As long as the weather permits and nuisance remains limited.

112498.explanation: This is part of your business operations.

112499.explanation: It is good that building is done as much as possible; we do not want to stand in the way of initiatives.

112500.explanation: This contributes to health and counteracts loneliness. It can also have a positive effect on participation in society.

112503.explanation: Not every child needs this subsidy. Make sure it is available to children who do need it.

112509.explanation: The central municipality of Arnhem provides for this.

112511.explanation: This is to give young people who have their roots in Doesburg the opportunity to settle in their birthplace.

112513.explanation: At the moment, many people already live permanently in holiday homes because no other housing is available. It is only logical to regulate this properly in practice.

112515.explanation: People should be encouraged to contribute socially. Coercion will have the opposite effect.

112516.explanation: Debt problems also cost the municipality a lot of money. Residents should receive what they are entitled to.

112517.explanation: People just above the minimum, who often work normally, also have difficulty making ends meet.

112519.explanation: Parking pressure is already high. Only allow where necessary because no other cars may be parked there, which is a waste of space.

112521.explanation: Appropriate help will solve problems more effectively.

112523.explanation: To the extent that it is increasing now, we are getting out of step with surrounding municipalities.

112526.explanation: We want more space for housing and green areas. It should also fit the character of the city.

112527.explanation: Give shopkeepers room to arrange this themselves.

112528.explanation: If the law requires it, we may not deviate from it.

112530.explanation: Fill the space on the roofs first. Do not use valuable space without solar panels.

112531.explanation: If we want to preserve a swimming pool, this is necessary.

112533.explanation: This causes a lot of nuisance. It lowers the threshold for children experimenting. So it should not be allowed.

112540.explanation: Residents must be able to reach their homes. Especially the elderly.

112541.explanation: Remaining independent is not feasible in the long term.

112542.explanation: This will counteract the housing shortage.

112543.explanation: First space for residents. Visitor parking should be as much as possible on the edge of the center.

112483.explanation: D66 stands for equal representation and active promotion of gender equality in governance.

112484.explanation: D66 wants more direct democracy, including through citizen forums.

112487.explanation: D66 supports multifunctional agriculture, but not 'always'; spatial quality remains leading.

112489.explanation: D66 carefully weighs economy, space, and sustainability; no absolute ban.

112491.explanation: D66 wants to enforce decent housing.

112493.explanation: D66 weighs health (fine dust) against individual freedom and therefore advocates for clean burning and regulation.

112498.explanation: D66 wants this in accordance with the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities for an inclusive society.

112499.explanation: D66 supports tailor-made solutions, with necessary spatial considerations.

112500.explanation: D66 wants to explore where there are opportunities for equal chances, health, and sports for everyone.

112503.explanation: D66 wants music education back in society.

112509.explanation: D66 focuses on combating domestic violence and femicide.

112513.explanation: D66 is open to carefully examining permanent residence to combat the housing shortage.

112516.explanation: D66 wants to fight poverty and not worsen it.

112517.explanation: D66 also wants to support people just above the poverty line.

112519.explanation: D66 encourages the electrification of society; this also requires good infrastructure for charging cars.

112521.explanation: Person-centered care is central to D66.

112523.explanation: We choose a livable municipality with strong facilities. Moreover, the property tax (OZB) is a relatively fair tax: those with a more expensive house pay more. This way, those with the greatest ability to pay contribute to good services.

112526.explanation: Dependent on sustainability, spatial integration, and the creation of employment opportunities.

112527.explanation: D66 gives entrepreneurs space and provides opportunities for economic growth.

112528.explanation: Building more is the solution to the housing shortage we face, not shifting priority.

112531.explanation: D66 values sports and physical activity.

112533.explanation: D66 wants regulation of soft drugs.

112540.explanation: Fits with livability and sustainable mobility.

112541.explanation: D66 positions itself as a reliable merger partner towards Westervoort, Duiven, and Zevenaar.

112542.explanation: This is the core point of D66's housing policy.

112543.explanation: D66 wants to manage mobility and land use.

112483.explanation: The quality of the leaders should be decisive, not their gender. However, we do want to see a good mix between both genders.

112484.explanation: A referendum can be useful for less complex matters. For complex issues, knowledge of the case is essential to make the right choices in the general interest. Reason versus Emotion.

112487.explanation: Stimulate entrepreneurship, including in the agricultural sector which already faces challenges.

112489.explanation: Entrepreneurship is good for the local economy and employment. If it fits within the environmental vision and complies with all legal standards, we are not opposed in principle.

112493.explanation: Attention must be given to the environment. We expect the operators to take responsibility themselves during fog and calm wind conditions.

112498.explanation: The municipality must stimulate and facilitate. For example, if the municipality needs to make adjustments in public spaces to enable this.

112499.explanation: Of course under certain restrictions, and it must not hinder other farmers in their operations.

112500.explanation: Participation of people in local society will always help to enable them to participate again and contribute actively.

112503.explanation: If the music lesson is given during school hours, we agree. A musical foundation broadens opportunities and is a great addition to technical lessons.

112509.explanation: Doesburg is small, so this would not be effective or safe. It is important that the municipality pays attention to 'Safe Home'. People affected by domestic violence must be helped.

112511.explanation: People who are socially and economically connected to Doesburg should be given priority. The VVD Doesburg motion 'Doesburg First' has tasked the council to work on this.

112513.explanation: In this time of housing shortage, we must be creative with possibilities. We do hope that enough homes will be added in the near future so that this will no longer be necessary.

112515.explanation: It is not only a reciprocal obligation but also gives a sense of self-worth. It is important to keep participating in society. Everyone has a talent, and we must encourage that as much as possible.

112516.explanation: In the various municipalities, policies differ; a nationwide uniform regulation should be introduced in place of these differences.

112517.explanation: In the various municipalities, policies differ; a nationwide uniform regulation should be introduced instead. VVD Doesburg believes that work should pay off.

112519.explanation: Depending on the demand. Our historic city center requires special attention when it comes to charging stations; the city's appearance must not be excessively disturbed by ugly charging stations. We must look for alternatives.

112521.explanation: Quality of care is a priority. Pillarization causes fragmentation and is undesirable in a time of labor shortages.

112523.explanation: Costs for residents should be kept as low as possible.

112526.explanation: There is hardly any space in Doesburg, but where possible and legally allowed, we are not opposed in principle.

112527.explanation: Especially on weekends, people have time to shop, so the municipality should give its full cooperation.

112528.explanation: We believe our Doesburg youth should be given priority for social rental housing. They are the future and often volunteer in many organizations. They also often provide informal care.

112530.explanation: VVD Doesburg believes this is not appropriate in Doesburg due to our small rural area. However, banning is too strong a word.

112531.explanation: VVD Doesburg considers preserving the swimming pool very important. Our motion 'A dive into the future' shows that we want to help the swimming pool stay afloat.

112533.explanation: For something like a coffee shop, it must be carefully considered whether it fits on the street. We believe a coffee shop does not enrich Doesburg.

112540.explanation: People and entrepreneurs must retain the possibility to access their premises. Car-restricted zones are a good alternative.

112541.explanation: Doesburg is no longer organizationally capable of executing all legal tasks. Although this is unfortunate, we must take our responsibility towards all our residents.

112542.explanation: No more cheap social rental housing; we already have 42% cheap rental homes. Affordable starter homes are very welcome and necessary.

112543.explanation: Due to lack of space in an old historic city center, it is unavoidable that payment is required for the few parking spaces. The rate is very reasonable.

112483.explanation: Diversity is important, but a fixed 50/50 standard does not always fit and should therefore not be a goal in itself. We choose the best aldermen based on quality and actively aim for broad representation.

112484.explanation: Residents should have influence, but a referendum is not necessarily the best tool for that. We choose real participation with village and neighborhood councils, citizen budgets, and good consultation in advance.

112487.explanation: CDA Doesburg wants to give farmers space for secondary activities such as a shop or campsite. This can contribute to a vital rural area and a fair income. However, we set conditions: it must fit the environment, cause no nuisance, and respect nature, traffic, and residents.

112489.explanation: Distribution centers provide relatively few jobs. Moreover, there are enough distribution centers in the municipality. We want more jobs for our own residents and therefore focus on sectors that can provide these, such as manufacturing, business services, and healthcare.

112491.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for decent housing for labor migrants. This can be on or near the company, but always with quality, supervision, and good agreements with neighbors.

112493.explanation: We do not ban things arbitrarily, but health comes first. Burning can only be responsible: no nuisance, clean burning, and when air quality is poor, we must be able to limit it. Under these conditions, the use of fireplaces and wood stoves should simply remain possible.

112498.explanation: CDA Doesburg finds accessibility important so that everyone can participate. The primary responsibility lies with the entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality supports with knowledge, rules, and setting an example, but structural financial contributions do not fit here.

112499.explanation: We understand the desire, but building on agricultural land cannot be unlimited. We look at each location to see what fits within the environmental plan, preserving the landscape and space for agriculture.

112500.explanation: Sports are important for health and social bonding. With the Gelrepas, Doesburg already makes sports participation possible for people with low incomes, including benefit recipients. CDA Doesburg supports this scheme and wants everyone to be able to participate easily.

112503.explanation: Music lessons are important for children's development and creativity. Therefore, we find it important that every child can have access to music lessons. We use subsidies targeted at children who otherwise could not afford them, in cooperation with schools and associations.

112509.explanation: Domestic violence is unacceptable and everyone deserves a safe home. Shelters must always be available. We prefer to organize this regionally and professionally. If a facility is needed in Doesburg, we will cooperate, ensuring good accessibility and aftercare.

112511.explanation: CDA Doesburg wants to build for its own residents. If starters are forced to leave and elderly cannot find suitable housing, villages weaken their identity and social cohesion. Therefore, local ties may play a role in allocating social rental homes, within the applicable rules.

112513.explanation: Permanent residence in recreational homes may seem a quick solution for lack of housing, but causes problems with safety, facilities, and enforcement. We solve the housing shortage with real housing construction and subdividing homes.

112515.explanation: Participation is important for people and society. CDA Doesburg finds it reasonable that a social benefit comes with a suitable counter-performance. This requires customization: we encourage socially meaningful and volunteer work, with good guidance and without unreasonable coercion.

112516.explanation: We will not let people with low incomes get into further trouble. Remission and debt assistance are necessary to prevent poverty and problematic debts.

112517.explanation: CDA Doesburg wants to help people in poverty but does not opt for raising income limits. We focus on customization, prevention, and targeted support. Thus, we help those in need, prevent poverty traps, and keep healthcare and social domains affordable.

112519.explanation: CDA Doesburg supports electric driving but is cautious about extra parking spaces as charging spots. That comes at the expense of scarce parking space. We encourage charging at logical locations, monitor the growth of electric driving, and seek a good balance between sustainability and accessibility.

112521.explanation: CDA Doesburg believes care should align with who people are and what they stand for. At the same time, it is not always feasible to guarantee full choice for everyone. Quality, accessibility, and continuity of care are paramount, with attention to faith and worldview where possible.

112523.explanation: We prefer to keep the property tax (OZB) low, but a reduction is currently not realistic. Investment in basic facilities, care for the vulnerable, and quality of life remains necessary. Therefore, we choose stability and predictability, with OZB increases no higher than inflation.

112526.explanation: Doesburg believes entrepreneurs need space to grow and provide jobs. Expansion of business parks is possible if done carefully. We prioritize restructuring and intensification and only expand if accessibility, sustainability, and living environment are well guaranteed.

112527.explanation: CDA Doesburg believes that shopping Sundays are not a goal in themselves. The initiative must come from entrepreneurs, not the municipality. Decisions about extra shopping Sundays should be made in consultation with entrepreneurs and residents, respecting peace, freedom of choice, and differences per locality.

112528.explanation: Refugees with residence permits should not automatically get priority; allocation must be fair and do justice to everyone who has been waiting a long time. Other municipalities already show that good temporary alternatives for status holders are possible.

112530.explanation: CDA Doesburg wants to continue the energy transition but not at the expense of agricultural land and nature. Solar panels should first go on roofs of homes, companies, and barns. As long as there is still ample space there, we find solar parks on agricultural land undesirable.

112531.explanation: Den Helder should remain and deserves support from the municipality. CDA Doesburg does not consider more money necessary.

112533.explanation: CDA Doesburg chooses a zero-tolerance policy: no coffee shops. They do not contribute to a healthy lifestyle and increase risks of addiction and mental problems. We protect vulnerable youth and focus on prevention, distance from schools and clubs, and strict enforcement.

112540.explanation: CDA Doesburg welcomes the current restrictions on car use in the center. Additional measures are not needed for now.

112541.explanation: The formation of a new merged municipality offers huge opportunities for Doesburg. Within a larger municipality, the city is better able to perform its tasks, provides more guarantee for retention of facilities, and is better able to guide its own future.

112542.explanation: CDA Doesburg strongly supports increasing the housing supply. In Doesburg's case, this does not have to be only in the affordable segment because it clearly has more of that than the national average. A balanced offering across different segments is desirable.

112543.explanation: CDA Doesburg prefers to reduce car use in the city center. Footpaths and cycle paths may be improved. A parking contribution may be requested from tourist visitors.

statements.title: The municipality must abolish paid parking in the center.
The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain allowed in Doesburg.

titleParts.text: In Doesburg moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The residents of Doesburg must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Doesburg moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must ensure that more affordable rental and owner-occupied homes are built.
There must be a women's shelter in Doesburg.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The business parks in Doesburg may be expanded.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Doesburg must give more money to the Den Helder swimming pool.
The center of Doesburg must become car-free.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Doesburg must remain an independent municipality.
